title: CLSR Activation Booth
subtitle: End-to-end concept, design, print production and on-site installation for a branded event activation.
role: Creative Lead, Designer and On-site Installer
summary: This project covered the full journey from brief to live experience: concept development, low-fi and high-fi prototyping, print-ready design production, and final on-site installation. The goal was to create a cohesive, high-impact activation with a kiosk and a prize wheel that felt premium and engaging for visitors.
brief: The assignment was to design and deliver a complete casino-themed activation setup for a live event. I led the creative process from initial concept to final execution, coordinated production with an external build vendor, and ensured all printed assets were production-ready and visually consistent across all touchpoints.
team: External production vendor for kiosk and prize wheel build
On-site collaboration during final installation and wrapping
comparisonsIntro: Final on-site results shown first, with the concept visuals used to plan and approve production.

live.caption: Installed bar and kiosk on site.

concept.caption: High-fidelity mockup of bar print application.

live.caption: Installed prize wheel at the event.

process.title: 1) Brief and creative direction
process.body: I translated the event brief into a clear visual direction and structure for the activation space. This phase focused on balancing brand presence, practical visitor flow and production feasibility.

process.title: 2) Lo-fi exploration and concept iterations
process.body: I explored multiple layouts and interaction ideas in low fidelity to validate format, hierarchy and visual storytelling before moving into detailed production assets.

images.caption: Early concept exploration for the prize wheel experience.
Alternative concept direction explored during ideation.

process.title: 3) High-fidelity 3D prototyping
process.body: The chosen concept was refined into high-fidelity mockups to preview scale, composition and branding in context. These visualizations were used to align expectations before print production.

process.title: 4) Print design and pre-press production
process.body: I produced complete print-ready assets for all surfaces and supporting materials, ensuring consistency in typography, color and messaging across the entire activation.

images.caption: A4 form and support material design.
Prize wheel segment and prize communication design.

process.title: 5) Vendor coordination and on-site installation
process.body: After approving final artwork, I ordered print production, coordinated with the fabrication partner, and took part in on-site wrapping and assembly to ensure quality in the final build.

result: The project delivered a fully coherent, production-ready activation setup with a strong visual identity from concept to execution. All core materials were finalized, printed and installed to support an engaging booth experience on-site.
